# Estimating Orange, RHD, 10+ Year Old Cars Worldwide

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Total cars worldwide
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars globally.

### 2. Right-hand-drive (RHD) car population
Key RHD markets (passenger cars):
| Market | Cars (millions) |
|--------|---------|
| Japan | ~62 |
| India | ~40 |
| UK | ~33 |
| Indonesia | ~15 |
| Australia | ~15 |
| Malaysia | ~13 |
| Thailand | ~10 |
| South Africa | ~7 |
| Others (Pakistan, NZ, Kenya, Sri Lanka, etc.) | ~20 |

**Total RHD ≈ 220 million** (~16% of the global fleet)

### 3. Fraction that are ≥10 years old
This varies by market — Japan's fleet turns over quickly (~40% are 10+ years), while developing RHD markets skew older (~60%). Weighted average across RHD countries: **~50%**

→ **~110 million** RHD cars aged 10+ years

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is consistently one of the rarest car colors. Modern data from PPG/Axalta shows orange at ~0.5–1% of *new* sales, but for cars manufactured pre-2015, orange was even rarer (~0.3–0.5%). Asian markets (dominant in RHD) skew heavily toward white/silver, pushing this lower. I estimate **~0.4%** of surviving 10+ year-old RHD cars are orange.

### Final calculation

$$110{,}000{,}000 \times 0.004 = 440{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 450,000**

# Estimating Orange, RHD Cars ≥ 10 Years Old

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Global car fleet
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars worldwide.

### 2. Right-hand-drive (RHD) fraction
Major RHD markets: Japan (~78M), UK (~33M), India (~50M), Australia (~15M), Thailand (~12M), Indonesia (~17M), Malaysia (~15M), South Africa (~12M), others (~25M) → **~260 million RHD cars (~18.5%)**.

### 3. Fraction at least 10 years old
Global median car age is roughly 10–12 years. Approximately **55%** of the global fleet is ≥10 years old. This holds reasonably for RHD countries as a group (Japan scraps early, but exports old cars to Africa/SE Asia; India's fleet is younger but growing).

→ RHD cars ≥10 years old: 260M × 0.55 ≈ **143 million**

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is consistently one of the rarest colors. PPG/Axalta global data puts orange at ~0.5–1% of **new** sales today, but 10+ years ago it was less trendy (~0.4–0.6%). In RHD-dominant markets (India, Japan, SE Asia), conservative color palettes push it lower.

I'll use **0.5%** for the 10+-year-old RHD cohort.

### 5. Final calculation

$$143{,}000{,}000 \times 0.005 = 715{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 750,000**
